title: Upper limb injuries: Elbow dislocation
body_html:
Epidemiology
  • 2nd most common large joint dislocation in the adult population after shoulder dislocation
  • Most commonly posterolateral

Mechanism
  • Posterior dislocations typically occur following a fall onto an extended arm

Classification
  • Simple: no associated fracture
  • Complex: dislocation with associated fracture

Associated fractures and complications
  • Radial head fracture most common -10% of dislocations
  • Coronoid process fracture
  • Terrible triad: elbow dislocation associated with a LCL complex tear, radial head fracture, and coronoid tip fracture
  • Rarely brachial artery injury encountered with fracture dislocations

Management
  • In simple dislocations closed reduction and a brief period of immobilisation in a sling are usually sufficient for good outcomes
  • Complex dislocations typically require operative management

XRAY showing posterior dislocation of the elbow
